_description: ERC AdG 2024 — Autonomic balance for adaptive decision making. Four-region DCM: dACC, AMY, VS, vACC. Three HGF-derived B-matrix modulators: epsilon_2 (reward PE), epsilon_3 (volatility PE), delta_hr (cardiac anticipatory signal).

A.label: A — Intrinsic
A.note: rows=target, cols=source. Self-inhibition A[i,i]=−0.5 (log-normal prior on decay).

B1.label: B₁ — ε₂ (H1)
B1.note: VS→dACC (row 0, col 2). ACh-VS incremental sampling pathway.

B2.label: B₂ — ε₃ (H2)
B2.note: AMY→dACC (row 0, col 1). NE-AMY phasic shift-detection pathway.

B3.label: B₃ — Δhr (H3)
B3.note: B₃[0,1]=+0.15 → bradycardia attenuates AMY→dACC (pos × neg = neg). B₃[0,2]=−0.20 → bradycardia boosts VS→dACC (neg × neg = pos).

C.label: C — Driving input
C.note: Outcome onset drives AMY (row 1) and VS (row 0 col 1 → row 1? check sign). C=0.25 each.

hypotheses.label: ε₂ → VS→dACC
hypotheses.short_desc: Outcome PEs scale VS→dACC — incremental sampling pathway
hypotheses.desc: When an outcome surprises the learner (large ε₂), VS sends a stronger incremental-value signal to dACC. The VS→dACC pathway scales proportionally with precision-weighted prediction error magnitude.

hypotheses.label: ε₃ → AMY→dACC
hypotheses.short_desc: Volatility PEs scale AMY→dACC — shift-detection pathway
hypotheses.desc: Unexpected contingency changes (large ε₃) trigger phasic AMY→dACC signalling. The amygdala detects that the world may have changed and increases its influence on the dACC conflict-integration hub.

hypotheses.label: Bradycardia ↑ VS→dACC
hypotheses.short_desc: Negative Δhr boosts VS→dACC via sign inversion (neg × neg = pos)
hypotheses.desc: Parasympathetic dominance (negative Δhr) enhances VS→dACC. At Δhr=−2: eff VS→dACC = 0.20 + (−0.20)(−2) = 0.60. Bradycardia opens the value pathway — the core HEART2ADAPT prediction.

hypotheses.label: Bradycardia ↓ AMY→dACC
hypotheses.short_desc: Negative Δhr attenuates AMY→dACC — threat pathway silenced
hypotheses.desc: Parasympathetic dominance attenuates AMY→dACC. At Δhr=−2: eff AMY→dACC = 0.30 + (0.15)(−2) = 0.00. Bradycardia quiets the threat pathway simultaneously with boosting VS.

hypotheses.label: STAI-T → tonic AMY↑ (A-matrix)
hypotheses.short_desc: PEB: STAI-T as continuous covariate on A[0,1] — tonic over-connectivity
hypotheses.desc: High trait anxiety predicts tonically stronger AMY→dACC (A[0,1]≈0.45 vs 0.30). Detected via PEB with STAI-T as a continuous covariate — analogous to the high-β_shk + high-ω phenotype.

hypotheses.label: High β_shk: B₃ attenuation
hypotheses.short_desc: PEB: high β_shk / STAI-T group predicts attenuated B₃ terms
hypotheses.desc: Even when high-β_shk participants achieve bradycardia, it fails to reconfigure the circuit. Both B₃ terms attenuated — the autonomic→neural coupling is broken. The v/n misattribution: noisy updating persists despite physiological calm.

hypotheses.label: Low β_rew → VS→dACC ↓
hypotheses.short_desc: PEB: β_rew predicts tonic A[0,2] and modulated B₁[0,2] — reward blunting
hypotheses.desc: Low β_rew (attenuated reward-driven approach) predicts weaker tonic VS→dACC (A[0,2]≈0.10 vs 0.20) and weaker ε₂ modulation. The reward-value signal is constitutively attenuated — separable from a learning deficit.

hypotheses.label: Both disrupted: AMY↑+VS↓+Δhr broken
hypotheses.short_desc: 4-group PEB: low β_rew + high β_shk + high ω — compound signature
hypotheses.desc: Compound disruption: A[0,1]=0.45 (AMY dominant), A[0,2]=0.10 (VS weak), both B₃ terms attenuated. Circuit is AMY-locked and unresponsive to autonomic state. Tests non-additive interaction (avoidance spiral).

phenotypes.label: Balanced
phenotypes.desc: β_rew=2.5, β_shk=2.5, ω=−3.0, γ=−0.3. Parasympathetic dominance (ACh-VS): ε₂→B₁[0,2] drives incremental VS→dACC sampling. NE-AMY balanced: ε₃→B₂[0,1] modulates phasic AMY→dACC. Bradycardia (Δhr)→B₃ reconfigures circuit: AMY↓ (B₃[0,1]) + VS↑ (B₃[0,2]). Balanced v/n ratio — noise correctly attributed to chance, volatility to environment. HGF μ₃ drops after volatile phase.

phenotypes.label: Low β_rew
phenotypes.desc: β_rew=0.8, β_shk=2.5, ω=−3.0, γ=−0.1. ACh-VS pathway selectively impaired: ε₂→B₁[0,2] reduced — reward PEs fail to drive VS→dACC incremental sampling. Threat pathway intact (ε₃→B₂, Δhr→B₃ functional). Learning rate ω unchanged — the problem is reward valuation, not volatility estimation.

phenotypes.label: High β_shk + High ω
phenotypes.desc: β_rew=2.5, β_shk=4.5, ω=−2.0, μ₃⁰=2.0, γ=−0.1. NE-AMY sympathetic overdrive: high ω → large ε₃(t) fluctuations → B₂[0,1] overdriven (AMY→dACC amplified). Failure to reach parasympathetic dominance: Δhr→B₃[0,1] attenuated — bradycardia cannot quiet AMY. High v/n ratio: noise misread as volatility, μ₃ stays elevated after volatile phase.

phenotypes.label: Low β_rew + High β_shk + High ω
phenotypes.desc: β_rew=0.8, β_shk=4.5, ω=−2.0, μ₃⁰=2.0, γ=−0.05. Full autonomic imbalance: ACh-VS pathway impaired (ε₂→B₁[0,2] reduced) + NE-AMY overdrive (ε₃→B₂[0,1] amplified). Δhr→B₃ fails both directions — bradycardia neither quiets AMY (B₃[0,1]) nor boosts VS (B₃[0,2]). Circuit locked AMY-dominant, unresponsive to autonomic reconfiguration.

drugs.label: Pilocarpine
drugs.crit_note: Critical test: does biperiden in healthy subjects produce the same DCM signature as the high-ω phenotype? If yes, the mechanism is cholinergic.

dacc.name: dACC — dorsal anterior cingulate cortex
dacc.role: Integration hub. Receives competing inputs from AMY (threat) and VS (value). Resolves approach-avoidance conflict via a competition between the two afferent pathways. Conflict output drives vACC for autonomic adjustment.
dacc.bold: BOLD ↑ during high-conflict trials. ψ₂ (effective learning rate) correlates with dACC activation. Key target for whole-brain GLM with ε₂/ε₃ as parametric modulators.

amy.name: AMY — amygdala
amy.role: Phasic threat and shift detection. Sends threat signal to dACC. Receives top-down regulation from vACC. Deep subcortical — primary reason fMRI is required over MEG/EEG.
amy.bold: BOLD ↑ on high-shock anticipation. B₂ > 0 predicts AMY BOLD scales with volatility PEs. TUS target: inhibitory TUS to AMY if A[0,1] is over-strong in anxiety group.

vs.name: VS — ventral striatum / NAcc
vs.role: Incremental reward prediction error signal. Drives approach behaviour via value-based updating. Deepest node — invisible to MEG/EEG. Core reason DCM over fMRI is needed.
vs.bold: BOLD tracks ε₂ PEs. B₁ > 0 predicts VS→dACC coupling scales with PE magnitude. Pharmacological target: pilocarpine (ACh agonist) expected to boost VS pathway (WP2).

vacc.name: vACC / sgACC — ventral/subgenual ACC
vacc.role: Top-down autonomic regulation node. Receives conflict signal from dACC; sends inhibitory feedback to AMY. Mediates interoceptive (cardiac/respiratory) state effects on the circuit.
vacc.bold: BOLD correlates with bradycardia magnitude. Weak vACC→AMY predicts reduced top-down regulation in high-β_shk participants. TUS excitatory target to enhance AMY inhibition.

peb.description: Two-layer parametric empirical Bayes. Layer 1: per-subject DCM θᵢ. Layer 2: θᵢ = μ + X·β + εᵢ.
